prevalence.population: Indian adults aged 45 and above (~600 million)
prevalence.source: Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) and Prospective Rural Urban Epidemiology (PURE) study, 2020-2023 updates
prevalence.trend: increasing due to rising cardiovascular risk factors, aging population, and improved survival from acute cardiac events

incidence.source: National Health Profile (NHP) by Central Bureau of Health Intelligence (CBHI), 2023

burden.source: Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation (IHME) Global Burden of Disease (GBD) Study, India 2021 estimates extrapolated

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Framingham criteria or ESC 2021 guidelines adapted for India, including echocardiography for ejection fraction <40% and clinical symptoms like dyspnea and fatigue

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Approximately 25% in urban areas, <10% in rural via Ayushman Bharat program
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 35% with effective management including guideline-directed medical therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Indian Society of Cardiology (ISC) guidelines, emphasizing echocardiography and BNP levels where available
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ESC/ACC guidelines adapted; beta-blockers, ACE inhibitors, ARNI, and diuretics widely available as generics; RHD-specific penicillin prophylaxis
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High burden of rheumatic heart disease in rural and tribal areas, limited access to advanced diagnostics in remote regions, air pollution exacerbating cases, and medication adherence issues due to cost
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Expansion of cardiac care under National Programme for Prevention and Control of Cardiovascular Diseases, affordable generic drugs, and increasing number of specialized heart failure clinics in urban centers
